requirements.title: Browse intersections and metadata
requirements.summary: Users can browse intersections and view basic intersection metadata.

source_refs.excerpt: Browse intersections
Grid/List view
Intersection name, ID, image

requirements.title: Playback saved simulation runs from Pinot
requirements.summary: Users can view and compare saved simulation runs sourced from Pinot.

source_refs.excerpt: View and compare saved simulation run from Pinot
Support playback of pre-simulated runs stored in Pinot.
Streaming saved simulation data from Pinot DB

requirements.title: Trigger and stream live simulations
requirements.summary: Users can trigger live simulator streams and receive live simulation data.

source_refs.excerpt: Trigger live simulator streams
Support triggering and streaming of live simulation runs.
Streaming live simulation data delivered via IoT Hub

requirements.title: Compare saved and live simulations through one integrated pipeline
requirements.summary: The system supports comparison of saved and live simulations and validates the integrated simulator-to-frontend pipeline.

source_refs.excerpt: Compare saved and live simulator streams
Validate the full integrated pipeline: Simulator → Pinot or SignalR → Traffic App Backend → Traffic App Frontend
Send both saved and live data to the frontend in the same way.

requirements.title: Model and persist intersection and simulation schema
requirements.summary: The backend models intersection schema data and persists schema-related data for simulations.

source_refs.excerpt: Schema modeling and make it as a source of truth.
Schema modeling for intersection schema data
Save in Cosmos DB as document.
Save the new simulation schema into SQL DB.

requirements.title: Expose backend endpoints for intersections, runs, and streaming
requirements.summary: The backend exposes endpoints for intersection listing, intersection detail, available runs, and saved and live streaming data.

source_refs.excerpt: Listing intersections
Fetching intersection detail
Fetching available simulation runs per intersection
Streaming saved simulation data from Pinot DB
Streaming live simulation data delivered via IoT Hub

requirements.title: Render intersection detail and playback controls
requirements.summary: The frontend provides intersection detail rendering, playback controls, live-mode start, and simulation configuration controls.

source_refs.excerpt: Intersection canvas view
Playback controls:
Toggle Live mode: Start new simulation
Configure the vehicle demand, timing plan and start simulation.

requirements.title: Support comparison views and performance validation
requirements.summary: The frontend supports side-by-side comparison views and validates rendering performance under high traffic and rapid phase changes.

source_refs.excerpt: Select two simulation runs (Live and Saved, or 2 saved ones)
Two / Four canvas panels side-by-side
Stress-test React + PixiJS canvas rendering performance for:
Identify React + PixiJS bottlenecks

risks.title: Dependency delivery delay can block planned delivery
risks.mitigation_note: If the dependency items were not delivered on time, it would be a risk for the planned delivery.

risks.title: External simulator and IoT dependencies may delay integrated testing
risks.mitigation_note: Deliver live simulator output stream into Traffic App backend.
